title: Publication scheme
body: "This publication scheme explains what information the Immigration Advice Authority (IAA) makes routinely available. It helps you find information about how we work, what we spend, and the services we offer.   We publish information as outlined in the Information Commissioner’s Office guidance.   This publication scheme is not an exhaustive list of individual publications, but rather a description of the classes or types of information that we are committed to publishing.  Who we are and what we do     About the IAA     How to contact the IAA    IAA’s leadership team    Governance structure     What we spend and how we spend it     Annual Reports and Accounts    Procurement policy   Sources of income     What our priorities are and how we are doing     Corporate Plan   Customer Service Charter     How we make decisions    Consultation documents and outcomes   Our policies and procedures     Commissioner’s code of standards    Guidance on competence    Complaints procedure    Complaints scheme (against immigration advisers)    How to make an FOI request    FOI Releases   Disclosure of information policy and procedure    Management of disclosures and disclosure information    Dealing with unacceptable behaviour towards IAA staff    Equality and diversity policy    Personal information charter    Criminal enforcement policy    Whistleblowing policy     Lists and registers     Register of regulated immigration advisers    List of regulated immigration advisers in your area    List of prohibited or suspended immigration advisers     The services we offer     Guidance for the public on immigration advice    Guidance for regulated advisers    Application forms and guidance on how to become an immigration adviser    Awareness materials (leaflets, posters, videos)    Press releases and communications     How to request information   Most information is available on the IAA website or GOV.UK. If you cannot find what you need, you can:   Submit an enquiry through the IAA Portal.   Post:  Immigration Advice Authority  PO BOX 567  Dartford Kent  DA1 9WX  United Kingdom    We aim to respond within 20 working days.   Feedback and complaints   You can give feedback, make a complaint about this scheme, or request an FOI response by submitting an enquiry through the IAA Portal.  If you’re not satisfied with our response, contact the Information Commissioner’s Office (ICO).   Accessibility   We aim to make all information accessible. If you need information in a different format, please submit a request through the  IAA Portal.  Crown copyright and licensing   Most documents supplied by the IAA will have been produced by government officials and will be protected by Crown copyright.   You may re-use the Crown copyright protected material (not including the Royal Arms and other departmental or agency logos) free of charge in any format for non-commercial research, private study or internal circulation within your organisation. The material must be acknowledged as Crown copyright, and you must give the title of the source document or publication.   Please see The National Archives website for more information on Crown copyright policy.   All the content on this website is under the Open Government Licence, except where otherwise stated.   Information supplied to you continues to be protected by the Copyright, Designs and Patents Act 1988. Therefore, information you receive which is not subject to Crown copyright continues to be protected by the copyright of the person, or organisation, from...
